name: oc20

data_origin.blend: DFT relaxations with PBE exchange-correlation functional

env_axis.name: composition
env_axis.shift_mechanism: held-out (adsorbate, catalyst) combinations; OOD-ads holds out adsorbates, OOD-cat holds out catalysts, OOD-both holds out both

ood_protocol.name: oc20-4way-validation
ood_protocol.ref: Chanussot et al. 2021 ACS Catalysis (dataset paper)

facts: 1.28M DFT relaxations of adsorbate-catalyst systems with PBE functional; published 4-way ID/OOD-ads/OOD-cat/OOD-both validation split.
hypotheses: OOD-both is a genuine compositional shift rather than interpolation in the (ads × cat) Cartesian product space.
The DFT functional choice acts as a hidden env factor, invisible inside OC20 but exposed only when paired with OC22.
intuitions: Open Catalyst likely appears in the counterfactual molecular / causal-discovery literature of 2024-25.
Meta FAIR probably has post-OC22 releases (a rumored OC25 or successor — status to confirm).
unknowns: The simulator-to-real-experiment gap is itself a candidate shift type, but there is no established label for 'simulation-to-experiment transitions' and no primitive in the schema for crossing it.
Whether 'catalyst' should be an atom-list or a bond-graph is a hidden representational choice the data presupposes.
epistemic_state.boundary: Simulator origin — claims about real-world OOD must lean on external chemical literature and cannot stand on OC20 alone.

representational_commitments: Catalyst represented as element-list + Miller index; bond-graph structure not exposed
Adsorbate-catalyst pair is the unit; multi-adsorbate configurations excluded
materialization_status: 48 GB downloaded via fairchem-core's download_data.py CLI per Dhruv's pointer. Includes the 4-way OOD validation splits (val_id, val_ood_ads, val_ood_cat, val_ood_both) per the canonical-split-only spec.
